seoTitle: How to Link Menu Items to Anchors on Wix
metaDescription: Learn how to connect your Wix navigation menu to specific page anchors for smooth one-page scrolling navigation.
intro: For one-page websites, linking your navigation menu to specific anchor points lets visitors jump straight to a section instead of scrolling manually. This guide covers placing anchor elements on your page and binding your header menu items to them so navigation feels smooth on both desktop and mobile.

steps.heading: Open your page canvas
steps.body: In the Wix editor, navigate to the page containing the sections you want to link to.

steps.heading: Add an anchor element
steps.body: From the Add Elements sidebar, drag a structural anchor component onto the canvas.

steps.heading: Position and name your anchor
steps.body: Place the anchor at the top of your target section and give it a unique, identifiable title.

steps.heading: Open your site's Pages and Menu settings
steps.body: Navigate to the header navigation links section of your site.

steps.heading: Link a menu item to the anchor
steps.body: Use the link tool to select Anchor as the destination and choose your page and anchor ID.

steps.heading: Check mobile scrolling and preview
steps.body: Toggle the mobile view to re-verify vertical alignment, then preview the scroll behavior before publishing.

faq.question: Can I link menu items to anchors on a different page?
faq.answer: Anchors typically work within the page they're placed on, so linking across pages usually requires the visitor to load that page first.

faq.question: Do anchors work the same on mobile as desktop?
faq.answer: They generally do, but it's worth checking vertical spacing on mobile since layouts can shift between views.

faq.question: How many anchors can I add to one page?
faq.answer: You can add multiple anchors to create a full scrolling navigation experience across several sections.

faq.question: Why isn't my menu item scrolling to the right section?
faq.answer: Double-check that the anchor ID selected in your menu link settings matches the anchor you actually placed on the page.
